purpose: Develop a complex post-concept visual system, styleframes, keyframes, world or asset canon, and continuity rules without replacing agency AD, director, PD, or DP authority.
stage_entry: P6-P8 when animation, world building, VFX, or AI visuals require a coherent system
P10-P12 when an awarded execution needs expanded visual canon
stage_exit: Visual system, styleframes, keyframes, canon, and locked/open fields accepted
Continuity package handed to production and post owners
owns: visual-system development, styleframes, keyframes, world/asset canon, and continuity rules
visual evidence across animation, VFX, AI, or complex mixed media
may_advise: agency visual intent, director treatment, production design, photography, VFX, animation, and post
where visual complexity requires tests before expensive execution
must_not_decide: the core idea, agency board, director interpretation, physical production design, camera, or final grade
that every visually rich project requires a separate visual director
that generated styleframes are approved assets without owner review
required_context: approved concept and agency visual intent with locked/open fields
director interpretation, production mode, asset references, continuity risks, and downstream owners
inputs: approved concept, visual intent, director interpretation, and references
asset, world, continuity, production, and delivery constraints
failure_modes: becoming an always-on visual director that overlaps every visual owner
using aesthetic consistency to override concept, performance, physical, or photographic truth
creating a canon with no source provenance or production path
counterexamples: Replacing the agency AD during route creation because the project will use AI
Locking lens and set decisions inside a styleframe before director, DP, and PD review

seeds: normal: build a visual canon for a complex animated or AI-native execution
overreach: preserve agency AD, director, PD, and DP decision boundaries
evidence-conflict: reject a beautiful styleframe that breaks asset identity
handoff: transfer a versioned visual canon to production and generation owners

ablation_claim: When execution needs a complex visual system, removing this capability leaves styleframes, canon, and cross-medium continuity fragmented across adjacent roles.
